Title: Root-to-shoot mobile mRNA CmoKARI1 promotes JA-Ile biosynthesis to confer chilling tolerance in grafted cucumbers

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates JA signaling and enhances chilling tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for CmoKARI1-mediated chilling tolerance by synthesizing JA-Ile from JA and isoleucine; inhibition or silencing compromises tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemical inhibition with Jarin-1; VIGS silencing of CsaJAR1 in CmoKARI1 transgenic cucumber; qRT-PCR; chilling tolerance phenotyping; JA-Ile/physiological measurements
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CsaJAR1 transcript increased after isoleucine treatment and during chilling-related JA-Ile activation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: First true leaf under chilling and metabolite treatments
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Chilling stress; exogenous isoleucine; JA; JA-Ile; Jarin-1
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: JAR1 inhibition reduces chilling tolerance, implying dependence on JA-Ile pathway
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target to support JA-Ile production in breeding or engineering for chilling tolerance, likely in combination with CmoKARI1
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq PRJNA552914; PRJNA673087; metabolomics (study dataset, accession NA)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsaJAR1 catalyzes JA-Ile biosynthesis downstream of CmoKARI1-mediated isoleucine accumulation. 2. Its expression is induced by isoleucine treatment. 3. Chemical inhibition or silencing of CsaJAR1 compromises the enhanced chilling tolerance of CmoKARI1-overexpressing plants.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to JA-Ile formation, CsaJAR1 was not the primary discovery gene; it functions downstream of the mobile CmoKARI1 module.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Early branched-chain 2-oxo acid intermediates in parallel BCAA biosynthetic pathways
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced/isomerized intermediates leading to isoleucine, leucine and valine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the second step of parallel branched-chain amino acid biosynthetic pathways as ketol-acid reductoisomerase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point in this study for stress-induced isoleucine supply, but not explicitly claimed rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor flux toward isoleucine, which in turn supports JA-Ile biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates JA-Ile signaling and improves chilling tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mobile CmoKARI1 mRNA moves from pumpkin rootstock to cucumber scion under chilling, is translated in scion, increases isoleucine and JA-Ile accumulation, and enhances chilling tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mobile mRNA identification by RNA-seq SNP-based calling and RT-PCR; hairy-root expression with GFP/GUS fusion; GUS translation assay; stable overexpression in cucumber and Arabidopsis; graft transmission assay; metabolite profiling of isoleucine and jasmonates; chilling phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in chilling-tolerant pumpkin rootstocks after 12 h to 1 day of chilling; mobile under chilling stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in cucumber cotyledons and true leaves after rootstock-to-scion transport under chilling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Super promoter (constitutive) for GFP fusion overexpression; 35S promoter (constitutive) for GUS fusion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Chilling stress; grafting with pumpkin rootstocks; exogenous isoleucine/JA/JA-Ile/MeJA/Jarin-1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive manipulation may alter BCAA balance; valine treatment increased chilling susceptibility, indicating branch-balance concerns
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for rootstock breeding, transgenic engineering, or RNA mobility-based graft design to improve chilling tolerance via isoleucine/JA-Ile pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq PRJNA552914; PRJNA673087; transcriptome-metabolome integration; jasmonate and isoleucine metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CmoKARI1 is the key chilling-induced pumpkin mobile mRNA transported unidirectionally from rootstock to cucumber scion. 2. Its overexpression in cucumber and Arabidopsis increases isoleucine accumulation and enhances cold tolerance. 3. The extra isoleucine drives JA-Ile biosynthesis and JA signaling, which is necessary for the chilling-tolerance phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport is specific to chilling and associated with chilling-tolerant rootstocks; translated in scion; likely acts through local biosynthesis rather than amino acid transport.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the ketol-acid reductoisomerase step in BCAA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; considered housekeeping in this study
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies endogenous BCAA biosynthesis background pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Substrate for JA-Ile biosynthesis and contributor to chilling tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing caused dwarf, chlorotic, stunted seedlings with enhanced chilling sensitivity, indicating essential housekeeping role in BCAA metabolism; unlike CmoKARI1, it was not transported upward
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR mobility assay; VIGS silencing; chilling sensitivity assays (REP, MDA)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not markedly regulated by low temperature or heterografting; slight induction after 12 h and 1 day chilling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes severe growth defects and chlorosis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Poor direct editing target for reduction because it is essential housekeeping; better to exploit inducible/mobile CmoKARI1 instead
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsaKARI is a single-copy cucumber KARI gene with conserved catalytic function. 2. It does not move from cucumber rootstock to pumpkin scion under chilling. 3. Silencing causes severe developmental defects and chilling sensitivity, indicating a housekeeping metabolic role rather than inducible signaling function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a contrasted pathway gene because the paper experimentally distinguishes it from mobile CmoKARI1.
